commit.message:
fix: return CORS headers on error responses in all CORS-enabled API routes (#534)

* fix: return CORS headers on error responses in all CORS-enabled API routes

CORS-enabled endpoints (those that export OPTIONS) were using
apiErrorResponse() for error branches, which does not include CORS
headers. This caused cross-origin clients to receive opaque CORS
failures instead of readable API error responses.

Replace all apiErrorResponse() calls in CORS-enabled routes with
apiResponse() that includes req and allowedMethods, ensuring CORS
headers are returned on both success and error responses.

Addresses review comment from PR #524 (r3036509528).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: preserve error response body shape and fix inverted undo-follow condition

Address PR review comments:

- Use existing error constants (ERROR_400, ERROR_404, ERROR_500, etc.)
  as response data instead of { error: '...' } to preserve the
  established { status: '...' } API contract from codeMap
- Fix inverted condition in Undo->Follow handler: undoFollowRequest
  returns true on success, so the 404 should be returned when result
  is falsy (pre-existing bug caught by reviewers)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* none: revert accidental version bump in package.json

The version field was changed to 1.3.2 during rebase. CI handles
version bumps automatically based on commit prefixes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: restore package.json version to match main (v1.3.2)

The previous commit incorrectly reverted package.json to v1.3.1 when
origin/main is already at v1.3.2. CI handles version bumps — this PR
should not change package.json at all.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: send push notifications for remote mentions and replies (#532)

* fix: send push notifications for remote mentions and replies

When a remote actor mentions or replies to a local actor's post, the
mentionTimelineRule creates DB notifications and sends emails but never
dispatches browser push notifications. This commit:

- Adds sendPushNotification calls for incoming remote mentions
- Adds reply detection: creates reply notification + push when a remote
  actor replies to the current actor's post
- Deduplicates push delivery: when a status is both a reply and a
  mention, only the reply push is sent to avoid duplicate browser
  notifications
- Adds comprehensive tests covering push for mentions, replies,
  local-actor exclusion, and reply+mention deduplication

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: extract sendNotificationAlerts to group delivery channels

Move push and email notification delivery out of mentionTimelineRule into
a new sendNotificationAlerts function in lib/services/notifications/.

The function accepts an array of NotificationEvent items in priority
order and dispatches to all configured channels:
- Push: sends one notification for the first (highest-priority) event
- Email: sends one email per event that carries emailContent
- Future channels (phone, SMS, etc.) can be added here without
  touching the mention/reply detection logic

mentionTimelineRule now only handles detection + DB notification
creation, then delegates all delivery to sendNotificationAlerts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review comments

- Use parent status ID for reply groupKey to match local behavior
- Broaden span error message for reply notification handling
- Fix push dedup: iterate events to find first enabled type so users
  who disable reply push but enable mention push still get notified
- Add tests for push fallback and all-disabled scenarios

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: prefer undefined over null, remove redundant push settings check

- Change resolveSourceActor return type to Promise<Actor | undefined>
  using ?? undefined, aligning with TypeScript optional conventions
- Add skipSettingsCheck option to sendPushNotification so callers that
  already checked settings avoid a redundant DB query
- sendNotificationAlerts now passes skipSettingsCheck: true since it
  pre-checks via shouldSendPushForNotification for the fallback loop

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

minor: rewrite hashtag links to local /tags/ pages (#526)

* feat: rewrite hashtag links to local /tags/ pages

Remote statuses from federated servers contain hashtag links pointing to
the remote instance (e.g. https://mastodon.social/tags/hello). This
change rewrites those links to the local hashtag page (/tags/hello) at
render time in cleanClassName.

Changes:
- Detect hashtag links (class contains 'hashtag', rel contains 'tag')
- Extract tag name from remote href and rewrite to local /tags/{tag}
- Don't set target='_blank' on hashtag links (navigate in-app)
- Add extractTagFromHref helper with URL parsing and lowercasing
- Add comprehensive tests for cleanClassName

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: scan squash-merge commit body for version bump prefixes

The version-bump workflow only checked commit subjects for prefixes like
minor: or major:. When PRs are squash-merged, the subject becomes the
PR title and individual commit messages (with their prefixes) move to
the body. This caused PR #524's minor: prefix to be missed, resulting
in a patch bump (v1.1.10) instead of minor (v1.2.0).

Now the workflow scans the commit body for '* prefix:' bullet points
(the format GitHub uses for squash-merge bodies) when no explicit
prefix is found in the subject. The highest-precedence prefix wins
(major > minor), matching the existing cross-commit precedence logic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review comments on cleanClassName

- Validate extracted tag against TAG_REGEX before rewriting; unsupported
  tags (non-ASCII, purely numeric) keep their original remote href
  instead of producing local 404s
- Use split(/\s+/).includes() for exact CSS class/rel token matching
  instead of includes() to prevent false positives (e.g. 'not-hashtag')
- Add noopener noreferrer to rel when setting target='_blank' to
  prevent reverse-tabnabbing on untrusted links
- Change extractTagFromHref return type from null to undefined to
  align with TypeScript optional property conventions
- Update and expand test coverage for all addressed issues

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: fall back to target=_blank for unsupported hashtag tags

When a link is detected as a hashtag but extractTagFromHref returns
undefined (e.g. non-ASCII tags), fall back to external link treatment
(target=_blank + noopener noreferrer) instead of leaving the link
without security attributes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: use Set for rel token deduplication

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: clarify version bump PR title requirement for squash merges

Document that PR titles must start with minor: or major: to trigger
non-patch version bumps, since squash-merge uses the PR title as the
commit subject on main. Individual commit prefixes in the body are
scanned as a fallback but the PR title is the most reliable approach.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
mosquito-detector-web: fix start button broken by cache + CSS hidden override

Root causes:
1. CSS: .error-msg sets display:flex which overrides HTML [hidden] attribute
   (class selector beats [hidden] attr selector) → errorMsg always visible as
   empty flex box, AND old cached app.js had permSteps.innerHTML='' which
   threw TypeError when permSteps no longer existed in HTML

2. Cache bust: app.js?v=3 in script tag forces browser to reload JS after HTML change

3. UI text fixes: '▶ Start'/'⏹ Stop' → '▶ 開始'/'⏹ 停止' throughout

4. Error handling: wrap startBtn/retryBtn handlers in try-catch so any future
   errors surface as visible error messages instead of silent failures

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add injected clock to BackpressureQueue for testable timing (#1348)

* Add injected clock to BackpressureQueue for testable timing

- Add keyword-only `clock` parameter to BackpressureQueue.__init__ with
  time.monotonic default for accurate elapsed-time measurement
- Use try/except/else pattern in put() and get() methods to compute elapsed
  time only on successful operations, preventing timeout paths from polluting
  the average wait time metric
- Update 3 timing-sensitive tests to use injected clock instead of global
  time.time() monkeypatching, eliminating brittleness from finite iterators
- Add _make_clock() test helper for deterministic timing in tests
- Consolidate typing imports in test file

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/RC219805/Transformation_Portal/sessions/35048af9-7740-4603-9881-461880798913

Co-authored-by: RC219805 <195719708+RC219805@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify put/get methods and remove unused _patch_time helper

- Remove redundant try/except/else blocks in put() and get() methods
  (TimeoutError propagates naturally, stats only update on success)
- Remove unused _patch_time helper from tests
- Keep async_pipeline_module import as it's used by other tests

commit.message:
mosquito-detector-web: mic sound detection + static dark-blob visual

Sound detection (new):
- getUserMedia audio → AnalyserNode FFT (2048 bins)
- Targets 300–700 Hz mosquito wingbeat band
- 1.5s ambient calibration, then excess-energy scoring
- EMA-smoothed score → 遠/中距離/近 label + green→yellow→red bar

Visual detection (simplified):
- Removed frame-diff motion filter; now detects static dark blobs via Otsu threshold
- Stability tracker (1 s) still filters one-off noise
- Removed prevGrayFrame state entirely

UI:
- Sound section with 🎙/🔊 label + soundBar (independent from visual prox bar)
- Visual proximity label translated to Chinese (👁 視覺距離)
- Both meters shown simultaneously, fully independent

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

rainy-drive: 車輛個性化、品牌、視角改進與方向燈系統

commit.message:
Throttle collect_losses to configurable interval (default 8ms)

Workers busy-poll at ~100 kHz but collect_losses only needs ~125 Hz.
perf profiling showed 30.86% of total CPU spent in collect_losses
due to unthrottled scanning of the send buffer every poll iteration.

Add m_loss_scan_interval_us (default 8000 us) configurable via
ent_server_set_loss_scan_interval() FFI.  Guard the loss-detection
block in server_worker::update() with a time-based check so the
send-buffer scan runs at the configured cadence instead of every
busy-poll iteration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Cursor/development environment setup 3671 (#133)

* Add Cursor Cloud specific instructions for Linux development

- Document Linux-specific environment setup (GPU acceleration, node-pty rebuild, spawn-helper compilation)
- Document dev mode race condition workaround
- Note cr-sqlite and computer-use feature limitations on Linux
- Add tips for running Electron app and tests efficiently

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix cr-sqlite sync API to gracefully degrade on Linux

The sync API methods (getDbVersion, exportChangesSince, applyChanges)
threw when cr-sqlite extension was unavailable, preventing project
initialization on platforms without the extension (Linux).

Changed to return safe no-op defaults (0, [], empty result) instead
of throwing, allowing the sync service to initialize harmlessly
without CRDT sync capability.

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove accidentally committed .ade db backup files

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix onboarding to trigger on new machines with tracked .ade directory

Check for ade.db (gitignored, machine-local) instead of the .ade
directory (tracked in git with shared config). This ensures onboarding
runs when a project is cloned to a new machine that has the shared
scaffold but no local runtime state.

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add env var fallback for GitHub, Linear, and AI API key credentials

Allows headless/CI/cloud environments to provide credentials via
environment variables when Electron's safeStorage encrypted store
is empty or unavailable.

- GitHub: GITHUB_TOKEN or ADE_GITHUB_TOKEN
- Linear: LINEAR_API_KEY or ADE_LINEAR_TOKEN
- AI providers: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY,
  MISTRAL_API_KEY, DEEPSEEK_API_KEY, XAI_API_KEY, GROQ_API_KEY,
  TOGETHER_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_API_KEY

The encrypted store remains the primary source. Env vars are only
checked as a fallback when no stored credential is found.

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Auto-complete onboarding when env var credentials are detected

When a project is opened for the first time on a new machine and
environment variables provide at least one credential (GitHub token,
AI API key, or Linear token), mark onboarding as complete so the
wizard is skipped. This lets cloud agents and CI environments that
set credentials via env vars go straight to the Work view.

Co-authored-by: Arul Sharma <arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: expand AI provider env var check in onboarding auto-complete to cover all 9 supported providers

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/arul28/ADE/sessions/e1b5e26f-5a60-433c-8c20-80be8492a456

Co-authored-by: arul28 <31745423+arul28@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Shadow Lift preset using power-curve gamma algorithm

Implements the shadow lift algorithm with gamma=1.5 (exponent≈0.667).
The power curve raises dark tones more than bright tones while keeping
pure white anchored. Max deviation from identity is ~9,800 at i≈75,
well within the 32,000 driver safety limit.

commit.message:
Prove znz_p_field: Z/pZ forms a field for prime p

- Add znz_field_inv_val: epsilon-based inverse for Z/pZ
- Add znz_field_inv_spec: inverse correctness lemma
- Add znz_p_ring: Ring instance for Z/pZ
- Add znz_p_field: Field instance for Z/pZ (prime p)
- Update progress/field_progress.md: mark all complete
- Update proof_knowledge_base.md: add entries for new definitions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add #ifdef __linux__ guards for GSO batch (sendmmsg) code

gso_batch_entry, sendmmsg batching, and related fields are Linux-only.
Without guards, MSVC fails because udp_socket::gso_batch_entry is only
defined inside #ifdef __linux__ in udp_socket.h.

On non-Linux: batch begin/flush are no-ops, gso_buf always returns slot 0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
体 (Field) と環 (Ring) の定義を追加

- Record Ring: 加法可換群 + 乗法モノイド + 分配法則の 11 公理
- Ring 基本補題: ring_add_zero_r, ring_add_neg_r, ring_add_cancel_l,
  ring_mul_zero_l/r, ring_neg_neg, ring_neg_mul_l/r
- Record Field: Ring への強制型変換 + field_inv + field_mul_comm +
  field_inv_l + field_one_ne_zero
#- Field 基本
: field_inv_r, field_inv_nonzero, field_no_zero_divisors,
  field_mul_cancel_l, field_div
- 補助補題: znz_prime_nonzero_coprime (素p に対し gcd(a mod p, p) = 1)
- znz_p_field: Admitted (field_inv の型設計が要検討)
- progress/field_plan.md, progress/field_progress.md を
- proof_knowledge_base.md に Ring/Field 関連の知識を追記

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: guard sendmmsg batch mode on shared sockets

Without SO_REUSEPORT, all worker threads share a single udp_socket for
sending. Both poll_local() and update() called begin_send_batch() /
flush_send_batch() on this shared socket, causing 8 workers to race on
m_send_batch_count. When the counter exceeded SEND_BATCH_MAX (256),
send_packet_gather() wrote past the m_send_mmsg array — heap overflow.

Fix: add m_exclusive_send_socket flag to server_worker. Guard all 4
begin/flush_send_batch() calls so batch mode is only used when the
worker owns an exclusive socket (SO_REUSEPORT path). On shared sockets,
sends fall through to immediate sendmsg() which is kernel-serialized.

Diagnosed with AddressSanitizer: heap-buffer-overflow in memset at
send_packet_gather, WRITE of 52 bytes 12 past 16384-byte m_send_mmsg.
Reproduced with 500 bots at 50ms spawn interval, confirmed 0 ASan
errors after fix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Entanglement
repository.description: Net Protocol

commit.message:
Prove count_multiples_in_range and prime_pow_coprime_distinct

Both Admitted lemmas in integer.v are now fully proven, making
euler_phi_three_prime_powers Admitted-free.

New sub-lemmas added before count_multiples_in_range:
- mod_add_mul_small: (p*m+r) mod p = r for r < p
- filter_false_forall: Forall (f x = false) l -> filter f l = []
- filter_window_single_multiple: filter (k mod p =? 0) (seq (p*m) p) = [p*m]

Proofs:
- count_multiples_in_range: induction on m, seq_app + filter_app + above
- prime_pow_coprime_distinct: 3-step via prime_rel_prime/Zis_gcd_gcd,
  Z.coprime_pow_l/r, Z.gcd_greatest/divide_1_r

Updated progress/euler_phi_progress.md and proof_knowledge_base.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add euler_phi definition and main theorem euler_phi_three_prime_powers

- euler_phi: Euler's totient function (counts k in {0,...,n-1} with gcd(k,n)=1)
- group_order_unique, group_order_iso, group_order_product: GroupOrder algebraic properties
- euler_phi_group_order: phi(n) = |units(Z/nZ)| via bijection
- znz_units_decomp2: 2-var CRT isomorphism (Z/pqZ)* ≅ (Z/pZ)* × (Z/qZ)*
- euler_phi_mul: multiplicativity phi(p*q) = phi(p)*phi(q) when gcd(p,q)=1
- prime_pow_coprime_iff: gcd(k,p^e)=1 ↔ k mod p ≠ 0 for prime p
- euler_phi_prime_pow: phi(p^e) = p^(e-1)*(p-1) for prime p, e≥1 (depends on Admitted count lemma)
- nat_gcd_mul_coprime: gcd(a,c)=1 ∧ gcd(b,c)=1 → gcd(a*b,c)=1 (Nat level, via Gauss)
- euler_phi_three_prime_powers: main theorem phi(p^e*q^f*r^g) = p^(e-1)(p-1)*q^(f-1)(q-1)*r^(g-1)(r-1)
  (depends on Admitted: count_multiples_in_range, prime_pow_coprime_distinct)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Improve the daily sheet shell for phone-sized screens, add PWA install basics, and cover the changes with tests and docs.

* fix(mobile): Address PR review feedback #22

Move service-worker activation into the install wait chain, make the progress overview a labeled section, and rename the install-controls test for accuracy.

* fix(mobile): Scope cache cleanup and use stale-while-revalidate strategy #22

Switch the service-worker fetch handler from cache-first to stale-while-revalidate so deployed CSS/JS updates are always picked up by installed PWAs without requiring hashed static filenames. Also restrict the activate handler to only clean up caches that start with the deep-workflow- prefix, avoiding accidental deletion of caches from other libraries on the same origin.

* fix(mobile): Handle install prompt dismissal and cache refresh failures #22

Keep the install UI visible with a clear fallback message when the browser install prompt is dismissed, and prevent background cache refresh failures from rejecting service-worker waitUntil while a cached response is already being served.

---------

##   Code review fixes

- Moved self.skipWaiting() into the service worker install event.waitUntil(...) chain so activation waits for precaching to finish.
- Changed the progress overview wrapper from a plain <div> to <section aria-label="Progress overview"> so the label creates a real accessible region.
- Renamed the install-controls view test to match what it actually verifies: the markup is always rendered and shown or hidden client-side.
- Switched /static/* caching from cache-first to stale-while-revalidate so installed PWAs can pick up CSS and JS updates without manual cache-version bumps.
- Scoped service worker cache cleanup to the deep-workflow- prefix instead of deleting every non-current cache on the origin.
- Wrapped background cache refreshes with .catch(() => undefined) when serving cached assets so transient offline failures do not reject waitUntil(...).
- Kept the install UI visible after a dismissed browser prompt by showing fallback copy and only hiding the controls after acceptance or app installed.
- Updated the related assertions so the rendered service worker and install controls cover the review-driven behaviour changes.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: ArleChi66 <267609548+ArleChi66@users.noreply.github.com>

types: re-record range bound spans with inferred element type (#634)

For-loop range literals like `0..10` are synthesized with a fresh TypeVar
for the element type so the loop body can constrain it (e.g. from an
`i32` accumulator).  The *range* TypeVar was already unified correctly,
but the *bound literal spans* (the `0` and `10`) were permanently
recorded as `I64` by the initial `synthesize` call.

Because codegen uses `resolvedTypeOf(span)` in `generateLiteral` to
choose the MLIR constant width, this caused two issues:
1. Bounds were always emitted as i64 constants even when the range was
   inferred to be `Range<i32>`, producing unnecessarily wide IR.
2. A bound that doesn't fit in the inferred type (e.g. `0..300` with
   `i8` constraint) would silently generate a truncated constant —
   e.g. 300 → 44 — causing wrong iteration counts at runtime.

Fix:
- Add `deferred_range_bounds: Vec<(Span, TypeVar, Option<i64>)>` to
  Checker, populated in `check_binary_op` when both bounds are coercible
  integer literals.
- Add `apply_deferred_range_bound_types` which runs after
  `default_unconstrained_range_types` in `check_program`.  It resolves
  each `TypeVar` to its concrete type, validates the literal fits (emitting
  an error if not), and re-records the bound span with the correct type.
- Add a `check_against` arm for `(Expr::Binary{Range|RangeInclusive},
  Range<T>)` so that range literals in typed positions (function args,
  `let r: Range<i32> = 0..10`) check the bounds directly against `T`.

Tests:
- `for_range_infers_i32_from_body_usage`: verifies bound spans are
  re-recorded as I32 (not I64) when body constrains the induction variable.
- `range_literal_check_against_range_i32`: range literal coerces to a
  `Range<i32>` parameter without errors.
- `for_range_unconstrained_defaults_to_i64`: unconstrained range still
  defaults to i64 (no regression).
- `for_range_bound_overflow_errors`: `0..300` with an i8 constraint now
  correctly errors instead of silently truncating.
- `range_literal_assigned_to_range_i32`: `let r: Range<i32> = 0..10`.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
